overall_summary: Excellent lap — you are ahead of the reference. Current gap: 9.670s faster than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 3 (+331.437s), where 14.1 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. Executing these fixes could recover 2.19s — a completely different lap time.

priority_actions.issue: Entry speed is 99.2 km/h below reference but apex speed is acceptable — over-braking on entry.
priority_actions.instruction: Trail the brakes into the corner rather than releasing fully at turn-in. Carry more entry speed and use trail braking to rotate the car to the apex.

priority_actions.issue: Entry speed is 96.4 km/h below reference but apex speed is acceptable — over-braking on entry.
priority_actions.instruction: Trail the brakes into the corner rather than releasing fully at turn-in. Carry more entry speed and use trail braking to rotate the car to the apex.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 7.8 km/h below reference (86.6 vs 94.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Carry more speed to the apex — target 94 km/h. Take a wider entry to enable a later apex and trust the exit. This corner is where straight-line speed is won or lost.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 86.6 km/h vs 94.4 km/h reference (-7.8 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Braking 10m too early compared to reference.
priority_actions.instruction: Move the brake marker 10m later. Reference brakes at 1335m, driver at 1325m.

sector_feedback.headline: Minimum corner speed is 7.8 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
sector_feedback.details: Average throttle application is 7% lower than reference — driver is hesitant getting back on power.
